An example of UX Widget processLayout() for Component, Entity, and Field widgets

processLayout()

For more complex widgets, it may be that the processLayout() API function works together with that of sub-widgets or of child widgets. For example, a component widget with entity widgets as its children, which in turn hold occurrence widgets, which in turn hold field widgets. In such situations, the processLayout() only generates a part of the layout corresponding to its own widget, and not for the child widgets. The child widgets should generate the layout of its own. After the parent widget's layout is generated, Uniface recursively invokes the processLayout() of the child widgets.

The widget hierarchy follows this pattern:

  • Component Level: Component widgets (binding: ucpt:)

  • Entity Level: Collection and occurrence widgets (bindings: uent:, uocc:)

  • Field Level: Field widgets (binding: ufld:)

Consider the following DSP layout scenario where all three widget are used: component widget, entity widget, and field widget. The example shows the layout generated by Uniface in Generated Layout Mode with component widget, where only the component-level binding ID is provided in the document for the DSP.

Generated Layout Mode:

<html>
   <head>
   </head>
   <body>
      <div id="ucpt:">
      </div>
   </body>
</html>

While generating the client-side layout, the processLayout() of the component widget class is invoked first based on the binding information ucpt:, which will result in the invocation of the processLayout() of the entity widget class and field widget eventually. Let us understand that in step-by-step order:

  • Component widget class processLayout() will generate the following, given that div is the required element's tag:
    <div id="ucpt:CUSTOMER_COMPONENT">
  <placeholder id="uent:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL"></placeholder>
  <placeholder id="uent:ORDER_ENT.NOMODEL"></placeholder>
</div>
  • Next, Uniface will detect a uent: binding and therefore will invoke the processLayout() of the corresponding collection widget class, which should generate the following:
    <div id="uent:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL">
  <placeholder id="uocc:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL"></placeholder>
</div>
  • Next, Uniface will detect a uocc: binding and therefore will invoke the processLayout() of the corresponding occurrence widget class, which should generate the following:
    <div id="uocc:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL">
  <placeholder id="ufld:UX_TEXTFLD.CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL"></placeholder>
</div>
  • Finally, Uniface will detect a ufld: binding and therefore will invoke the processLayout() of the corresponding UX field's widget class, which should generate the following:
    <fluent-text-field id="ufld:UX_TEXTFLD.CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL" class="outline">...
</fluent-text-field>

The complete layout generation will be following:

<div id="ucpt:CUSTOMER_COMPONENT">
  <div id="uent:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL">
    <div id="uocc:CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL">
      <fluent-text-field id="ufld:UX_TEXTFLD.CUSTOMER_ENT.NOMODEL" class="outline">...
      </fluent-text-field>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="uent:ORDER_ENT.NOMODEL">
    <div id="uocc:ORDER_ENT.NOMODEL">
      <fluent-text-field id="ufld:ORDER_ID.ORDER_ENT.NOMODEL" class="outline">...
      </fluent-text-field>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Examples

Custom component example:

class CustomComponent {
  static processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) {
    let widgetElement;
    if (skeletonWidgetElement.tagName.toLowerCase() === "div") {
      // Use the existing div element from the skeleton
      widgetElement = skeletonWidgetElement;
    } else {
      // Create a new div element for the component container
      widgetElement = document.createElement("div");
    }
    // Add component-specific styling
    widgetElement.className = "custom-component";
    widgetElement.style.border = "2px solid #0078D4";
    widgetElement.style.padding = "1em";
    // Generate child entity placeholders for recursive processing
    renderChildDefinitions(widgetElement, objectDefinition);
    return widgetElement;
  }
}

Custom collection example:

...
class CustomCollection {
  static processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) {
    let widgetElement;
    if (skeletonWidgetElement.tagName.toLowerCase() === "fluent-data-grid") {
      // Use the existing fluent-data-grid element from the skeleton
      widgetElement = skeletonWidgetElement;
    } else {
      // Create a new div element for the collection container
      widgetElement = document.createElement("div");
    }
    // Add collection-specific styling
    widgetElement.className = "custom-entity-collection";
    // Generate occurrence placeholders for recursive processing
    renderChildDefinitions(widgetElement, objectDefinition);

    // Copy all supported declarative occurrence‑level properties from the collection widget to its occurrence widgets.
    let occ_prop_ids = []; // Add your occurrence property IDs here
    // Example: let occ_prop_ids = ["row-backgroundColor", "row-isEnabled"];
    // Alternatively get it from occurrence widget.
    if (occ_prop_ids.length > 0) {
      // Create a Set for efficient property name lookup
      const propertyNamesSet = new Set(objectDefinition.getPropertyNames());
      const occurrenceProperties = {};

      // This filters the properties and collects only those that are defined in the objectDefinition.
      for (let i = 0; i < occ_prop_ids.length; i++) {
        const prop = occ_prop_ids[i];
        if (propertyNamesSet.has(prop)) {
          occurrenceProperties[prop] = objectDefinition.getProperty(prop);
        }
      }
      // set the collected properties as occurrence-level properties.
      if (Object.keys(occurrenceProperties).length > 0) {
        objectDefinition.setOccurrenceProperties(occurrenceProperties);
      }
    }

    return widgetElement;
  }
}...

Custom occurrence example:

...
 class CustomOccurrence {
  static processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) {
    let widgetElement;
    if (skeletonWidgetElement.tagName.toLowerCase() === "div") {
      // Use the existing div element from the skeleton
      widgetElement = skeletonWidgetElement;
    } else {
      // Create a new div element for the occurrence container
      widgetElement = document.createElement("div");
    }
    // Add occurrence-specific styling
    widgetElement.className = "custom-occurrence-row";
    // Generate field placeholders for recursive processing
    renderChildDefinitions(widgetElement, objectDefinition);
    return widgetElement;
  }
}
...

Custom field example:

class CustomField {
  static processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) {
    let widgetElement;
    if (skeletonWidgetElement.tagName.toLowerCase() === "fluent-text-field") {
      // Use the existing fluent-text-field element from the skeleton
      widgetElement = skeletonWidgetElement;
    } else {
      // Create a new fluent-text-field element for the field widget
      widgetElement = document.createElement("fluent-text-field");
      // Copy the id from skeleton element
      widgetElement.id = skeletonWidgetElement.id;
    }
    // Add field-specific properties
    widgetElement.className = "outline";
    // Note: Field widgets are leaf nodes - no renderChildDefinitions() call needed
    return widgetElement;
  }
}

Title link
Uniface UX entity widgets

Related Links
  • [webentitywidgets]

    The [webentitywidgets] section of the initialization file contains logical widget definitions for entities used in Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components.

  • [webcomponentwidgets]

    The [webcomponentwidgets] section of the initialization file contains logical widget definitions for components used in Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components.

  • processLayout

    A function that informs a widget to provide a processed initial HTML layout to be used at a run-time.
